ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #2, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar Aug-09-2023 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: Roger Gross appearing via zoom for Michael Armentrout (administrator).

Discussion was held regarding the extraordinary fees, that Mr. Quinn was estranged from his family and died intestate, and hat Mr. Gross spent a significant time locating 43 heirs. First amended petition for final distribution on second account and third and final report etc., is taken under submission.

Judge: Stephen Murphy. Clerk: A. Datt. Deputy: Singh. Not reported.

Off Calendar Jun-06-2022 Continued To Jun-27-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Roger Gross, via Zoom, for Michael Armentrout, Administrator.

The court and counsel discuss the status of birth certificates as evidence to establish Wanda Lackey's issues. Counsel reports of a formal request to an heir search company and is waiting for their reply. The court points to the questionnaires submitted by counsel and advises that it would like to see the birth certificates.

After additional discussion, the matter is continued to Jun-27-2022 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204.

Judge: Hon. Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Bastain. Not Reported.

Petition For Determination Of Entitlement To Estate Distribution
Off Calendar Dec-20-2021 Continued To Mar-14-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Roger Gross, Esq. counsel for Michael Armentrout, Petitioner (appearing by video conference).

At the Court's invitation, Mr. Gross reports that contrary to the Estate's understanding, a pre-deceased uncle of the decedent in fact had six children as opposed to none. At last count, there were 74 people entitled to notice and 44 heirs at law. An heirship report is pending but is not expected until February 2022.

Mr. Gross confirms the Court's assessment that the current petition is not in a position to be granted and further advises that an amended petition will need to be filed. The Court queries Mr. Gross as to the viability of hearings scheduled for January 2022. Mr. Gross advises that these matters should remain on calendar but that the heirship petition now before the Court should be continued.

After discussion, the Court continues the matter to March 14, 2022 at 9:00 a.m. on Dept. 204.

Judge: Hon. Ross C. Moody; Clerk: R. Michael Diles; Bailiff: Deputy Dillingham. Reporter: Not Reported. (D204)

PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
Off Calendar Sep-14-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Roger Gross, via ZOOM, for Michael Armentrout, Personal Representative; James Van Duzen, in person, Interested Party.

The court begins by querying counsel as to the status of the two-year accounting. Mr. Gross reports that he had not realized that the accounting petition had been rejected and asks for continuance to prepare and re-submit an accounting. The court then queries Mr. Gross as to why there has not been a petition filed to increase the bond in accordance with local rule and why the Inventory and Appraisal alleges that the bond is sufficient. Mr. Gross reports that the main asset of the estate had been real property which has been sold. Proceeds from the sale were used to pay off the remaining mortgage and outstanding taxes which have reduced the remaining assets and that the existing bond should be sufficient. Mr. Gross advises that the preceding will be outlined in the accounting petition. The court then confirms with Mr. Gross that he will provide new notice to all interested parties.

The Accounting Status Petition is submitted. The Status of Filing of Two-Year Accounting is continued to Oct-13-2021 at 9:00 am in Department 204. The Status of Bond is off calendar.

Judge: Hon. Vedica Puri. Probate Examiner: Brandon Slykhous. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: F. Ruiz. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Bond
Off Calendar Sep-13-2021 Off Calendar

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Filing Of Two-Year Accounting
Off Calendar Sep-13-2021 Continued To Oct-13-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
Off Calendar Jul-26-2021 Continued To Sep-13-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Roger Gross, via ZOOM, for Michael Armentrout, Personal Representative.

The court begins by advising counsel of a few possible notice issues. Counsel reports mailing proof of service regarding today's hearing on 7/05/2021 which listed Trudy Lister. Counsel is queried regarding service to Brandi VanDusen Tarabochia. Counsel reports that she had been removed from the service list as it has been determined that she is not an heir. Counsel advises that he had mailed a letter to Brandi requesting a copy of her birth certificate to confirm that she is the stepdaughter of one of the heirs but that he never received a reply. The court advises counsel that the preceding is not good cause to exclude her from the service list and reminds counsel of the importance of rectifying notice issues and instructs counsel to include Brandi in any future notices filed with the court regarding this matter. The court then turns to whether counsel believes the current bond is sufficient. Counsel reports that the current bond seems sufficient and provides testimony to support his position.

After additional discussion, the Probate Accounting Status Petition, Status of Filing of Two-Year Accounting, Status of Bond, and Petition for Determination, etc., are continued to September 13, 2021, at 9:00 am in Department 204.

Judge: Hon. Ross Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: R. Loo. Not reported.

PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
Off Calendar May-03-2021 Continued To Jun-21-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Roger Gross, via ZOOM, counsel for Michael Armentrout, Administrator.

At the outset, the court requests a status of this matter. Mr. Gross reports that several issues have prohibited the estate from closing. Mr. Gross reports that income taxes had not been filed from 2012 up to the date of the decedent's death. There were proceeds from the sale of a condominium which were used to pay more than $550,000 in taxes owed by the decedent. The remaining funds are less than what had been reported on the Final Inventory and Appraisal previously filed with the court. The court then advises that the Status of Administration filed in 2020 never mentioned the intent to file a Petition for Determination of Entitlement to Estate Distribution. Counsel reports that he did not realize that the estate could not close due to the Petition for Determination of Entitlement to Estate Distribution but advises that an Accounting Petition is ready to file. The court advises that it will be ordering counsel to submit a two (2) year accounting, from Sep-28-2018 to Sep-28-2020, pursuant to Probate Code 10950, as a stand-alone petition. Additionally, the court orders counsel to the file the necessary papers to update the court with the current value of the estate by either filing an ex parte application to fix the bond issue or by filing an amended Inventory and Appraisal.

The Accounting Status Petition is continued to June 21, 2021 at 9:00 am in Department 204. Also, a Status for the filing of a 2-year Accounting and a Status of Bond will be added to calendar on June 21, 2021 at 9:00 am in Department 204. The Petition for Determination of Entitlement to Estate is continued from June 15, 2021 to June 21, 2021 at 9:00 am in Department 204, in open court.

Judge: Hon. Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Kabasares. Not reported.
